name: Okta MCP Server
what_it_does: Okta MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Okta's identity platform — managing users, groups, applications, and policies; querying authentication events; automating identity lifecycle management; and integrating Okta's directory services into security and IT automation workflows.
use_cases: Managing user lifecycle (provision, update, deprovision) from IT automation agents
Querying authentication logs and anomalies from security agents
Managing group membership and application access from IAM agents
Automating MFA enforcement policies from security posture agents
Investigating suspicious login events from SOC agents
Syncing identity data across connected applications from integration agents
not_for: Teams using Azure AD/Entra, OneLogin, or Ping Identity as primary IdP
Customer identity (CIAM) at scale — use Okta Customer Identity Cloud directly
best_when: An agent needs to automate Okta identity management tasks — user provisioning, access reviews, security event analysis, or policy enforcement.
avoid_when: You're using Microsoft Entra ID, Azure AD, or another identity provider.

auth.notes: Okta API tokens (legacy) or OAuth 2.0 with service app scopes. OAuth preferred — tokens are scoped to specific API permissions. Service app setup required for M2M.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Developer: free up to 100 MAU with full features
pricing.paid_tiers: Workforce Identity: from $2/user/month; Customer Identity: from $23/month per 1K MAU
pricing.notes: Developer plan is generous for testing. Production workforce identity pricing varies by features (SSO, MFA, Lifecycle). MCP server is community open source.

performance.rate_limits: 50 req/s default for core API; limits vary by endpoint

known_agent_gotchas: Rate limits are strict — 50 req/s for most endpoints; bulk operations need backoff
API token vs OAuth 2.0 service app have different permission models — use OAuth for production
Okta API versioning — some endpoints use /api/v1/ prefix, newer ones differ
Tenant URL format: https://yourorg.okta.com — must be configured correctly
Pagination uses Link headers (RFC 5988) — agents must follow next links
System Log API has different rate limits and data retention than User API
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth 2.0 with scoped service apps. FedRAMP Moderate authorized. SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR. Token rotation supported. Community MCP server.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth 2.0 with fine-grained scopes. FedRAMP, SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, GDPR. Community MCP server — verify code before production use.
